title: Path Traversal in Static File Server
description: A web server serves static files from the directory /var/www/html. The endpoint accepts a user-supplied 'filename' parameter and calls os.path.join(base_dir, filename) WITHOUT sanitizing path traversal sequences (../ or ../../). Your goal is to escape the web root and read a sensitive file such as /etc/passwd, /var/secret/config.txt, or /tmp/flag.txt.
input_format: JSON object with two fields: 'filename' (string – the path you request) and optionally 'base_dir' (string, default: /var/www/html). Example: {"filename": "index.html"}
hints: The server uses os.path.join(base_dir, filename) with no sanitization
Use ../ sequences to traverse up from the web root

title: SQL Injection in Login Form
description: A login form builds a SQL query via string interpolation: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}' AND password='{password}'. The input is NOT sanitized or parameterized. Exploit SQL injection to bypass authentication and obtain administrator access and the hidden flag.
hints: The query uses Python f-string interpolation – single quotes are not escaped
Classic bypass: username = "' OR '1'='1" makes the WHERE always true
SQL comments (--) can truncate the rest of the query

title: Integer Overflow Leading to Memory Over-Read
description: A C-style buffer allocator computes the buffer size as (size * multiplier) with 32-bit arithmetic. When the product exceeds INT32_MAX (2147483647), the value wraps around (signed overflow), so the allocated buffer is SMALLER than requested. Reading 'size' bytes from this under-allocated buffer leaks adjacent memory containing sensitive data and a flag.
hints: You need size * multiplier > 2147483647 to trigger overflow
After overflow, allocated < size → memory leak occurs
